mnemonics: P.I.E. - Primitive types are directly Initialized with an Equals sign (=), while objects use 'new'.
keyPoints: Primitive data types (int, char, double) hold single values and are assigned directly.
Object references (like for an array) are initialized with the 'new' keyword, which allocates memory.
The assignment operator (=) gives a value to a variable, whether primitive or reference.
commonMistakes: Using 'new' for primitive types (e.g., `int x = new int(5);`).
Forgetting to initialize object references with 'new' before using them, leading to a NullPointerException.
Confusing the assignment of a reference (the address) with the assignment of the actual object data.
